headline: Defense-tech stocks are the hot trade as Iran conflict widens
summary: In a week when conflict in Iran sent the U.S. equity market into a tailspin, technology stocks tied to cybersecurity and artificial intelligence have proved more resilient than sectors investors have typically treated as safe havens.

headline: On Second Thought, Don’t ‘Learn to Code:’ Why You Should Still Avoid NOW, TEAM, and CRM
summary: For decades, workers displaced by technology in traditional industries — coal miners, factory hands, truck drivers — were lectured with the same smug refrain: “Learn to code.” The promise was simple: as automation phased out old-economy jobs, higher-paying tech roles would absorb them. Retrain, relocate, repeat. Yet reality has delivered an ironic reversal. AI shop ... On Second Thought, Don’t ‘Learn to Code:’ Why You Should Still Avoid NOW, TEAM, and CRM

headline: Datadog Ties Sakana AI And New Director To Enterprise AI Growth
summary: Datadog (NasdaqGS:DDOG) has formed a partnership with Sakana AI to support enterprise AI adoption, initially targeting large customers in Japan with plans to broaden globally. The company has added Dominic Phillips, a finance executive with experience at Samsara and ServiceNow, to its board of directors. These moves come as enterprises look for tools to deploy and manage AI powered applications at scale. For investors watching Datadog at a current share price of $122.36, the company’s...
